report_name: Foxconn Hit by Nitrogen Ransomware; Attackers Claim 8TB of Data from Apple, Google, Intel Projects

threat_actors.name: Nitrogen
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that emerged in 2023, known for targeting large enterprises in the manufacturing, technology, and construction sectors. The group employs a double-extortion model, encrypting files and exfiltrating data to pressure victims into paying a ransom. They claimed responsibility for the attack on Foxconn's North American facilities.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration, with a focus on stealing valuable intellectual property.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen group exfiltrated 8 terabytes of data by compressing and uploading it to attacker-controlled cloud storage as part of their double-extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen ransomware payload was deployed across Foxconn's network to encrypt files, disrupt operations, and extort a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: As part of their typical attack chain, the Nitrogen group likely deleted or disabled backups and Volume Shadow Copies to prevent easy restoration from encrypted files.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely performed network mapping to identify high-value systems and data stores within the Foxconn network before exfiltration and encryption.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely searched file servers and other systems to locate sensitive project files, drawings, and hardware schematics for exfiltration.

malware.name: Nitrogen Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Nitrogen group to encrypt files on Foxconn's systems. The attack is part of a double-extortion scheme involving data theft.

tools.name: vssadmin.exe
tools.description: The vssadmin.exe utility was likely used by the attackers to delete Volume Shadow Copies to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: Foxconn
identities.description: The primary victim of the ransomware attack. Foxconn is the world's largest electronics contract manufacturer and a critical supplier for global tech companies. The attack impacted its North American facilities.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential project files, drawings, and hardware schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential project files, drawings, and hardware schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Intel
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential project files, drawings, and hardware schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Nvidia
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential project files, drawings, and hardware schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

infrastructure.name: Nitrogen Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Nitrogen ransomware group to claim responsibility for the attack and threaten to leak stolen data.

infrastructure.name: Nitrogen Exfiltration Cloud Storage
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled cloud storage infrastructure used to host the 8 terabytes of data allegedly exfiltrated from Foxconn's network.

indicators.name: Nitrogen Ransomware VSS Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e7f-4b3a-8c1d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware like Nitrogen to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/cyber-risk/foxconn-attack-highlights-manufacturings-cyber-crisis
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of `vssadmin.exe` to delete all volume shadow copies quietly, a common ransomware behavior to prevent system recovery.
